title: Token warning threshold cast uses lossy f32 to f64 conversion with cast_possible_truncation allow
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/proxy.rs:629-648
## Violation
record_turn_estimate computes utilization with two awkward integer/float casts:

  let utilization_pct_x10 = estimated_input.saturating_mul(1000).checked_div(context_window).unwrap_or(0);
  let usage_pct_f64 = f64::from(utilization_pct_x10 as u32) / 10.0;
  ...
  let threshold_tokens = (f64::from(context_window as u32) * f64::from(warn_threshold)) as usize;

For context_window > u32::MAX (a future model with 5B-token context), the as u32 truncates silently. warn_threshold is f32 but used in f64 arithmetic — a sub-percent threshold loses ~7 digits of precision. The allow(cast_possible_truncation) annotation is the LARP that masks a real future bug.
## Mandated refactor
Compute threshold via integer math: threshold_tokens = context_window * (warn_threshold * 1000.0).round() as usize / 1000. Use u128 if guarding against future context-window growth.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
